feedback.text: Companies have long reminded us ad nauseum of their Terms of Service. But they have never kept their side of treating us well in an ever predatory market that grows ever so harsher. As such it is time for the tables to finally flip. We need to remind companies that just as they have expectations and rules for us we have some for them as well. STOP nickel and diming us with your predatory loot boxes, microtransactions, useless DLC and pre order bonuses. STOP telling us what we are allowed to do with your games, whether that be modding it or even extracting it for preserving it. And on that note, STOP demanding that we stop preserving works of art that inspire us when you give us proof time and time again that you could not care any less. Gamers can be a lot of things. Controversial? Sure. Vocal? Absolutely. Embarrassing? To the biggest degree. I mean, I look at what I'm writing and I'm beet red. But all of this comes from one thing the gaming industry and its leader lack completely. Passion I'm tired of having my pockets ran dry from a hobby that used to be known for how accessible it was. Tired from feeling like I'm in a business meeting every time i read another game's ToS as if it isn't the 5th one of that month. Give us our voice back. Listen to our feedback. Go back to appreciating my money instead of just coveting them and maybe I will give it to them.
